title: ADAR RNA-sensor basal leak sits near 1-3% of the induced ('on') signal

context.assay: ADAR RNA sensor (CellREADR/RADARS) payload reporter expression
context.condition: basal (no-target) expression relative to full target-induced expression

title: RADARS (Jiang et al. 2023) achieved up to ~277-fold reporter activation

context.assay: RADARS ADAR sensor (Jiang et al., Nat Biotechnol 2023)

title: A somatic point mutation carries 2 bits of information (one of four bases at one position)

context.condition: information content of a single-nucleotide substitution among 4 possible bases (log2(4)), before any channel bottleneck

schema_fit.constraint: measurement_basis only distinguishes an empirically measured physical quantity (FUNDAMENTAL) from a computed ratio/fold-change over experimental data (DERIVED); it has no category for an analytically derived information-theoretic constant (log2(4) possible bases at one position) that is neither instrument-measured nor a data-derived ratio.
schema_fit.current_ir_behavior: Forced into FUNDAMENTAL with unit='bits' so the required unit field is non-null, even though no physical measurement was performed.
schema_fit.candidate_resolution: Add a third measurement_basis value (e.g. 'ANALYTIC') for constants derived from combinatorics/information theory rather than instrumentation or data reduction.
schema_fit.purity_cost: Additive enum value on measurement_basis; existing FUNDAMENTAL/DERIVED entries are untouched and byte-identical.

title: A DNA base pair spans ~0.34 nm within a ~3.2-billion-bp genome

context.condition: rise per base pair in chromatinized double-stranded DNA, against a ~3.2-billion-bp genome (the '0.34 nm-in-3.2 Gb problem' for live genomic-DNA SNV imaging)

title: ~77% of TET2 somatic lesions are truncating mutations

context.condition: TET2 somatic lesion spectrum; truncating class is exactly the NMD-susceptible class that degrades before an RNA sensor can act on it

schema_fit.constraint: leaf.context only allows tissue/cell_line/assay/condition sub-keys; there is no 'gene' or 'target_locus' key to record that this 77% figure is a property of the TET2 locus specifically, not of a tissue, cell line, or assay.
schema_fit.current_ir_behavior: Gene identity is folded into the free-text condition string as a workaround, so it is not a structured, queryable field.
schema_fit.candidate_resolution: Add an optional 'gene' (or 'target_locus') sub-key to leaf.context for claims whose defining stratification variable is a gene/locus rather than a tissue or cell line.
schema_fit.purity_cost: Additive optional context sub-key; existing entries that omit it are untouched and byte-identical.

title: Catalytic reporting, not thermodynamic binding, is the tractable strategy for live-nucleus single-base sensing

leaf.data: A chromophore that physically binds a single genomic base would have to win a thermodynamic discrimination contest -- the free-energy gap between a perfectly matched probe and a single-mismatch duplex -- against body-temperature noise, inside a chromatinized, protein-occluded nucleus, at single-copy abundance.
leaf.warrant: The escape is to replace binding with catalytic reporting: an enzyme (ADAR, Cas13) performs a sequence-specific reaction only when its guide finds the cognate target, and that reaction gates the synthesis of an amplifiable output, so the cell becomes its own signal amplifier.

title: ADAR's nearest-neighbor sequence preference (the CCA edit-context rule) gates which SNVs a sensor can discriminate

leaf.data: ADAR disfavors a 5' guanosine adjacent to the target adenosine (5' preference U > A > C > G) and favors a 3' G (3' preference G > C ~ A > U); sensor designs exploit a triggering C-A mismatch at the edit site and the design heuristic that the target supply an effective CCA at the editing position.
leaf.warrant: Because ADAR editing efficiency is dictated by nearest-neighbor sequence context (mapped to catalytic-domain residues E488/T490/V493), the discriminating base of a variant-sensing guide must sit at or adjacent to the editing-critical position, where a single mismatch maximally perturbs editing -- so not every somatic SNV falls in a compatible context.
leaf.rebuttal: A specific steric clash with a 5' guanosine is sometimes attributed to a flipping-loop glycine, but the chapter flags this exact mechanistic detail as unverified against the primary structural literature.

title: RNA-sensor detectability is asymmetric: strong for missense/in-frame variants, weak for NMD-prone truncating LOF variants

leaf.data: Truncating mutations (nonsense, frameshift) introduce premature termination codons that flag the transcript for nonsense-mediated decay (NMD), destroying the very mutant mRNA the sensor needs; RNA sensing is strongest for missense and in-frame variants (stable full-length transcript, one clean discriminating base) and weakest for the truncating LOF lesions that dominate tumor-suppressor genes.
leaf.warrant: An ADAR/Cas13 sensor can only report a transcript that survives to be translated; because NMD selectively degrades premature-termination-codon-bearing mRNA before the reporter payload can be produced, truncating/frameshift variants systematically evade RNA-based genotype sensing while missense variants remain detectable.
leaf.rebuttal: A substantial minority of NMD substrates escape (last-exon and very-5' PTCs are spared) and NMD efficiency varies by transcript, tissue, and individual; the often-quoted '~one-third escape' figure is an approximation, not a fixed rate, and is unverified as a universal figure.

title: Counterintuitive-applications narrative: a translated sensor payload can be swapped for any therapeutic effector
Narrative aside: the epigenome as the sensing frontier's 'white space'
